mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from 0.08 in early 2022 to 0.33 by April 2022, marking the start of a hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends coincide with positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% vintage April 2022, near cycle lows, historically a leading indicator of turning points
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of triggered UNRATE-rise instances, or the triggered sample has fewer than 15 historical occurrences making the signal untestable.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation and a rising FEDFUNDS rate jointly indicate an aggressive real-tightening regime where the Fed is compelled to hike faster than inflation cools, compounding valuation pressure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 288.663 in April 2022 reflecting elevated inflation alongside FEDFUNDS hiking from near-zero
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows forward 20d returns not significantly different from single-factor FEDFUNDS-only signal (interaction term coefficient not significant at p<0.10 in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in HOUST (housing starts) signals that higher borrowing costs are suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand, which propagates to broader consumption and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days specifically when FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising; this relationship is expected to be muted or absent in easing or flat-rate regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1793.0 declining to revised 1712.0 in March 2022 amid rising FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: The HOUST-decline signal produces no significant difference in forward 60d returns between tightening and non-tightening regimes (interaction effect not significant), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered tightening-regime instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate production capacity, which feeds directly into near-term earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 104.5853 for March 2022 reflecting continued industrial expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM growth and forward 5d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative INDPRO growth periods do not show correspondingly worse forward returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS (retail sales) growth exceeds a 0.5% month-over-month threshold, it signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value 667724.0 for March 2022 indicating continued nominal retail sales growth despite inflation
failure_condition: Triggered RSAFS growth episodes fail to produce forward 20d returns significantly above the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating PAYEMS growth combined with a simultaneous uptick in UNRATE jointly indicates a genuine labor market inflection rather than noisy monthly variation, since PAYEMS alone is subject to high revision risk; this joint deterioration signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days that are stronger than either series predicts independently.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates by > 50% AND UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 151314.0 vs revised 151623.0 for April 2022 showing revision uncertainty alongside UNRATE at 3.6%
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient between PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise is not statistically significant (p>0.10) relative to single-factor models, or joint-trigger sample produces forward 60d positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy begins to bind meaningfully on credit conditions and equity valuations, producing a discrete negative shift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days that is absent when rates remain below this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.33 in April 2022 with market expectations of rapid hikes toward and beyond 2.5% through 2022
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns above and below the 2.5% FEDFUNDS threshold are not statistically distinguishable (difference-in-means p > 0.10) across historical hiking cycles.
